div1.entryFree: Balance, subst., a pair of scales to weigh things: a mote will turn the b. Mids. V, 324. Not inflected in the plural: Are there b. here to weigh the flesh? I have them ready. Merch. IV, 1, 255 (cf. sense, Mcb. V, 1, 29. Oth. IV, 3, 95. Antipholus, Err. V, 357). Metaphorically: many likelihoods which hung so tottering in the b. All's I, 3, 130. to whom I promise a counterpoise, if not to thy estate a b. more replete, II, 3, 183. in the b. of great Bolingbroke are all the English peers, R2 III, 4, 87. H4B IV, 1, 67. Tit. I, 55. Oth. I, 3, 330. -- Attribute of justice: Ado V, 1, 212. H4B V, 2, 103.
